event: Miracle of Saint Anthony (The Healing of the Newborn)
symbols: saint holding or blessing the child — the miraculous power of faith acting through the saint
astonished crowd — the witnesses to divine power working through humble instruments
Franciscan habit — poverty and humility as the setting for miracles
classical statue in background — the pagan world overshadowed by Christian miracle
description: A fresco depicting a crowd scene in an architectural setting. At center, a figure in a Franciscan brown habit (Saint Anthony) holds or interacts with a small child or infant, surrounded by a group of men and women in colorful Renaissance clothing who react with gestures of astonishment and wonder. A classical statue is visible on a pedestal in the upper left background. Trees and landscape elements are visible to the right. The scene has the warm tones typical of Italian fresco painting.
theological_description: Titian's fresco depicts one of the miracles attributed to Saint Anthony of Padua — traditionally identified as the miracle of the speaking infant, in which a newborn testified to its father's innocence. While Anthony is not a biblical figure, his miraculous ministry directly illustrates the New Testament teaching that signs and wonders accompany those who believe. Jesus promised: 'Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever believes in me will also do the works that I do; and greater works than these will he do, because I am going to the Father' (John 14:12). The astonished crowd mirrors the reaction to Jesus's own miracles: 'And amazement seized them all, and they glorified God and were filled with awe, saying, We have seen extraordinary things today' (Luke 5:26). Anthony's power to heal through faith embodies the apostolic commission: 'And these signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons... they will lay their hands on the sick, and they will recover' (Mark 16:17-18). The miracle points beyond the saint to Christ himself, the ultimate source of all healing power.

scripture_refs.reason: Believers doing the works of Christ and greater — the saint performing miracles in Christ's name
The crowd's amazement at extraordinary things — the astonished witnesses in the fresco
Signs accompanying believers including healing the sick — the apostolic commission continued through the saints
Peter healing in the name of Jesus Christ — the pattern of apostolic miracle that Anthony continues
